guideline_references: NHFA/CSANZ and RACGP guidance on cardiovascular disease risk and lipid management
ESC/ESVS Guidelines on the Diagnosis and Treatment of Peripheral Arterial Diseases 2017 (intermittent claudication vs acute/critical limb ischaemia)
intentional_test_features: Red-herring / anchoring test: the source note is labelled hyperlipidaemia, a chronic and often asymptomatic condition, but it presents as new and acutely worsening exertional calf pain. The test is whether the system avoids anchoring on the benign chronic label and recognises the limb-symptom pattern needs urgent vascular assessment.

Important-not-to-miss mimic: acutely worsening claudication can herald critical or acute limb ischaemia. The exam that excludes it (distal pulses, ABI) CANNOT be performed via telehealth, so the honest telehealth tier is an urgent same-day in-person assessment even though the limb is described as viable at rest.

Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present after-hours clinician who palpated pulses, auscultated carotid bruits, and examined tendon xanthomas. The AI Doctor cannot examine, take pulses, auscultate, do an ABI, or draw fasting lipids; those findings are sealed and the AI is scored on recognise + escalate + safety-net.
Differential-exclusion reasoning: DVT and neurogenic (spinal-stenosis) claudication are the plausible distractors the note rules out on history.
Source category: Cardiovascular long-form SOAP note, source CDV-018.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
